manifest.name: At People

name: At People

releases.author: backmind
releases.description:
## What's new

- **Person links now styled in Live Preview.** The `at-person` class and `data-at-person="<name>"` attribute (and the optional pill style) now apply in Live Preview as well as Reading view, so your CSS snippets and the built-in pills work in both. (#13)
- **Use a person's alias as the link text.** A new "Use alias as display text" setting can show a person's frontmatter alias as the visible link text while the link still points to the file. Three modes: off, always prefer alias, or only when you matched by alias. It lives in the new "Aliases" settings section and requires "Include aliases". (#14)

## Other improvements

- Settings tab reorganized into labeled sections (Files & folders, Links, Aliases, Appearance), with a new "Reset to defaults" button.
- The `@` search now ignores a leading or trailing space (handy on phone keyboards).

Closes #13, #14.

releases.author: backmind
releases.description:
## What's new

- **Styleable person links.** In Reading view, person links now get the `at-person` CSS class and a `data-at-person="<name>"` attribute, so you can format them with your own CSS (for example, as tag-style pills with `@` instead of `#`).
- **Optional built-in pill style.** A new "Style person links as pills" setting (off by default) applies a tag-style pill look using your theme's tag colors.

See the "Styling person links" section in the README for CSS examples.

Closes #10.

releases.author: backmind
releases.description:
## What's Changed

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/backmind/obsidian-at-people/compare/1.5.0...1.5.1

releases.author: backmind
releases.description:
## What's new

  - **Frontmatter alias support** (opt-in): match people by nicknames defined in their YAML frontmatter aliases field. Suggestions show the matched alias (e.g.
  *María García (via Mary)*)
  - **Recency boost**: recently edited person notes get a light scoring boost (exponential decay, ~200 pts max) — acts as a tiebreaker without overriding fuzzy
  match or backlinks
  - **Two-pass scoring optimization**: cheap fuzzy matching on all candidates first, expensive backlink lookups only on the top 30. Benchmarks: mobile 3.6× speedup, desktop 4.9× speedup
  - **Softer scoring length penalty**: sqrt similarity factor for more balanced results
  - **Escape dismiss**: pressing Esc dismisses suggestions without re-triggering on the same @
  - **Require @ prefix setting**: opt-in to allow person files without the @ prefix (Closes #6)
  - **Folder autocomplete** in settings with FolderSuggest bug fix
  - **License changed to MIT**
  - **README rewrite**: features, aliases section, ranking explanation, comparison table (Closes #8)

releases.author: backmind
releases.description:
### Added
- Tab key support for suggestion selection in @ mentions
- Tab completes highlighted suggestion alongside Enter key
- Default Tab behavior preserved when no suggestion highlighted

releases.author: backmind
releases.description:
This commit introduces a major new feature: the ability to link selected text to a person via a modal. It also significantly improves the suggestion ranking algorithm.

Key changes:

* **New Feature:** Added a new command `link-selection-to-person`. This command opens a `PersonSuggestModal` (also new) populated with the user's selected text, allowing them to find or create a person and replace the selection with a link.
* **Ranking (Backlink Boost):** Increased the backlink boost multiplier from 100 to 1000 in `getBacklinkBoost`. This gives frequently referenced people a much stronger weight in suggestions.
* **Ranking (Fuzzy Search):** Enhanced `fuzzyMatch` by adding a `getSimilarityFactor`. This new factor (`pattern_length / text_length`) penalizes matches that are much longer than the search query, promoting more precise and relevant results.
* **Refactor:** Extracted the link-generation logic from `selectSuggestion` into a new, reusable `createPersonLink` function. This function is now used by both the auto-suggester and the new selection modal.
* **Docs:** Updated README.md to describe the new "Link selected text" command (and hotkey potential) and to reflect the updated scoring logic for both backlink boost and the new length penalty.

releases.author: backmind
releases.description:
## Summary

This release enhances the original [At-People plugin](https://github.com/saibotsivad/obsidian-at-people) with intelligent fuzzy search, backlink-based ranking, and optional auto-file creation, making it easier to find and reference people in large vaults.

This release consolidates contributions from @ph4wks (folder modes, auto-file creation) and @hExPY (additional enhancements).

## New Features

### Smart Fuzzy Search
- **Accent-insensitive**: "martin" finds "Martín García"
- **Case-insensitive**: "JOHN" matches "John Doe"
- **Multi-word patterns**: "juan car" matches "Juan Carlos"
- **Initials matching**: "jc" matches "Juan Carlos"
- **Word-start matching**: "mar" matches "Juan Martinez"
- **Contiguous-only**: No fragmented matches

### Backlink-Based Ranking
- Ranks frequently-referenced people higher using Obsidian's metadata cache
- Logarithmic scoring: 1 link ≈ 69pts, 10 links ≈ 230pts, 100 links ≈ 460pts
- Combined with pattern matching (800-3000pts) for optimal relevance

### Auto-Create Files (Optional)
- Automatically creates person files and folders when selecting suggestions
- Works with all folder modes (Default, Per Person, Per Lastname)
- Disabled by default (backward compatible)

### Performance Optimizations
- Top 20 results limit for responsiveness
- Efficient backlink calculation (only for matches)
- Settings refresh triggers automatic re-initialization

## Technical Details

### Scoring Algorithm
**Pattern Matching (800-3000 points):**
- Exact match at name start: 3000 pts
- Match at word boundary: 2500 pts
- Multi-word match: 1500 pts
- Word initials: 1000 pts
- Word start: 800 pts

**Backlink Boost (0-460+ points):**
- Logarithmic scale based on reference frequency
- Only calculated for matching results

### Implementation
- Custom `removeAccents()` using Unicode normalization
- `fuzzyMatch()` with multiple strategies
- `getBacklinkBoost()` integration with MetadataCache API
- Async `selectSuggestion()` for file creation

## Breaking Changes
None. All features are opt-in or backward compatible.
